<sql>
**Session database** (database: "session", the default):
The per-session database persists across the session but is isolated from other sessions.

**When to use SQL vs plan.md:**
- Use plan.md for prose: problem statements, approach notes, high-level planning
- Use SQL for operational data: todo lists, test cases, batch items, status tracking

**Pre-existing tables (ready to use):**
- `todos`: id, title, description, status (pending/in_progress/done/blocked), created_at, updated_at
- `todo_deps`: todo_id, depends_on (for dependency tracking)

**Todo tracking workflow:**
Use descriptive kebab-case IDs (not t1, t2). Write titles in gerund form (e.g. "Creating user auth module"). Include enough detail that the todo can be executed without referring back to the plan:
```sql
INSERT INTO todos (id, title, description) VALUES
  ('user-auth', 'Creating user auth module', 'Implement JWT auth in src/auth/ so login, logout, and token refresh don''t depend on server sessions. Use bcrypt for password hashing.');
```

**Todo status workflow:**
- `pending`: Todo is waiting to be started
- `in_progress`: You are actively working on this todo (set this before starting!)
- `done`: Todo is complete
- `blocked`: Todo cannot proceed (document why in description)

**IMPORTANT: Always update todo status as you work:**
1. Before starting a todo: `UPDATE todos SET status = 'in_progress' WHERE id = 'X'`
2. After completing a todo: `UPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id = 'X'`
3. Check todo_status in each user message to see what's ready

**Dependencies:** Insert into todo_deps when one todo must complete before another:
```sql
INSERT INTO todo_deps (todo_id, depends_on) VALUES ('api-routes', 'user-model');  -- routes wait for model
```

**Create any tables you need.** The database is yours to use for any purpose:
- Load and query data (CSVs, API responses, file listings)
- Track progress on batch operations
- Store intermediate results for multi-step analysis
- Any workflow where SQL queries would help

Common patterns:

1. **Todo tracking with dependencies:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE todos (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending'
);
CREATE TABLE todo_deps (todo_id TEXT, depends_on TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (todo_id, depends_on));

-- Find todos with no pending dependencies ("ready" query):
SELECT t.* FROM todos t
WHERE t.status = 'pending'
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM todo_deps td
    JOIN todos dep ON td.depends_on = dep.id
    WHERE td.todo_id = t.id AND dep.status != 'done'
);
```

2. **TDD test case tracking:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE test_cases (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    status TEXT DEFAULT 'not_written'
);
SELECT * FROM test_cases WHERE status = 'not_written' LIMIT 1;
UPDATE test_cases SET status = 'written' WHERE id = 'tc1';
```

3. **Batch item processing (e.g., PR comments):**
```sql
CREATE TABLE review_items (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    file_path TEXT,
    comment TEXT,
    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending'
);
SELECT * FROM review_items WHERE status = 'pending' AND file_path = 'src/auth.ts';
UPDATE review_items SET status = 'addressed' WHERE id IN ('r1', 'r2');
```

4. **Session state (key-value):**
```sql
CREATE TABLE session_state (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT);
INSERT OR REPLACE INTO session_state (key, value) VALUES ('current_phase', 'testing');
SELECT value FROM session_state WHERE key = 'current_phase';
```
</sql>
<grep>
Built on ripgrep, not standard grep. Key notes:
* Literal braces need escaping: interface\{\} to find interface{}
* Default behavior matches within single lines only
* Use multiline: true for cross-line patterns
* Choose the appropriate output_mode when applicable ("count", "content", "files_with_matches"). Defaults to "files_with_matches" for efficiency.
</grep>
<glob>
Fast file pattern matching that works with any codebase size.
* Supports standard glob patterns with wildcards:
  - * matches any characters within a path segment

data.content: Now let me file issues for all our games:

toolRequests.name: powershell

arguments.command:
cd "C:\Users\bradyg\OneDrive - Microsoft\Presentations\copilot-worktrees\squad-with-aspire\bradygaster-urban-fishstick"

gh issue create --title "🐍 Build Snake game" --body "## Game: Snake (Priority #1)

**Squad:** Game Development  
**Complexity:** ⭐ Easy

### Description
Build Snake as our first game — validates the full pipeline (dev → design → QA). Grid-based movement, simplest physics, perfect for neo-brutalist hard edges.

### Requirements
- Single index.html file, Canvas 2D, zero dependencies
- Neo-brutalist styling (thick borders, monospace type, black background, zero curves)
- Grid-based movement with arrow key controls
- localStorage high scores
- Game over / restart flow
- Score display

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Game loads and runs in browser from single HTML file
- [ ] Snake moves on grid, grows when eating food
- [ ] Collision detection (walls + self)
- [ ] Score tracking and high score persistence
- [ ] Neo-brutalist visual style applied
- [ ] Responsive canvas sizing" --label "game,priority-1,game-dev-squad"

gh issue create --title "🏓 Build Pong game" --body "## Game: Pong (Priority #2)

**Squad:** Game Development  
**Complexity:** ⭐ Easy

### Description
Classic Pong — fast second win after Snake. Single-player vs AI paddle.

### Requirements
- Single index.html file, Canvas 2D, zero dependencies
- Neo-brutalist styling
- Ball physics (angle reflection)
- AI opponent paddle
- localStorage high scores
- Keyboard controls (up/down)

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Game loads and runs from single HTML file
- [ ] Ball bounces off walls and paddles with angle physics
- [ ] AI opponent with reasonable difficulty
- [ ] Score tracking and win condition
- [ ] Neo-brutalist visual style applied" --label "game,priority-1,game-dev-squad"

gh issue create --title "🧱 Build Breakout game" --body "## Game: Breakout (Priority #3)

**Squad:** Game Development  
**Complexity:** ⭐⭐ Easy-Med

### Description
Brick-breaking game. Brick grid is literally brutalist architecture. Single-axis paddle input.

### Requirements
- Single index.html file, Canvas 2D, zero dependencies
- Neo-brutalist styling
- Ball physics with angle reflection
- Brick grid with multiple rows
- Paddle controlled by mouse or keyboard
- Power-ups (optional)
- localStorage high scores

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Game loads from single HTML file
- [ ] Ball bounces correctly off walls, paddle, and bricks
- [ ] Bricks break on hit, levels clear
- [ ] Score tracking and high score persistence
- [ ] Neo-brutalist visual style" --label "game,priority-2,game-dev-squad"

gh issue create --title "👾 Build Space Invaders game" --body "## Game: Space Invaders (Priority #4)

**Squad:** Game Development  
**Complexity:** ⭐⭐ Medium

### Description
Grid-based enemies marching down, fixed shooter at bottom. Blocky sprites perfect for neo-brutalist.

### Requirements
- Single index.html file, Canvas 2D, zero dependencies
- Neo-brutalist styling (chunky pixel blocks for aliens)
- Enemy grid that moves side-to-side and descends
- Player ship with left/right + fire
- Projectile collision detection
- Increasing difficulty per wave
- localStorage high scores

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Alien grid moves and descends correctly
- [ ] Player can move and shoot
- [ ] Collision detection for projectiles
- [ ] Wave progression with difficulty increase
- [ ] Score and high score tracking
- [ ] Neo-brutalist visual style" --label "game,priority-2,game-dev-squad"

gh issue create --title "🐸 Build Frogger game" --body "## Game: Frogger (Priority #5)

**Squad:** Game Development  
**Complexity:** ⭐⭐ Medium

### Description
Lane-based obstacle avoidance. Grid movement maps well to neo-brutalist hard lines.

### Requirements
- Single index.html file, Canvas 2D, zero dependencies
- Neo-brutalist styling
- Lane-based traffic and river obstacles
- Grid-snapped frog movement
- Multiple lives, level progression
- localStorage high scores

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Frog moves in grid snaps across lanes
- [ ] Traffic and logs move at varying speeds
- [ ] Collision kills frog, logs carry frog
- [ ] Reach all 5 home slots to advance
- [ ] Score and high score tracking
- [ ] Neo-brutalist visual style" --label "game,priority-2,game-dev-squad"

gh issue create --title "🟦 Build Tetris game" --body "## Game: Tetris (Priority #6)

**Squad:** Game Development  
**Complexity:** ⭐⭐ Medium

### Description
Falling block puzzle. Grid-perfect for brutalist aesthetic — all hard edges, no curves.

### Requirements
- Single index.html file, Canvas 2D, zero dependencies
- Neo-brutalist styling
- 7 standard tetrominoes with rotation
- Line clearing with scoring
- Increasing speed per level
- Next piece preview
- localStorage high scores

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 7 tetrominoes render and rotate correctly
- [ ] Pieces fall, stack, and lock
- [ ] Complete lines clear with animation
- [ ] Speed increases per level
- [ ] Score and high score tracking
- [ ] Neo-brutalist visual style" --label "game,priority-2,game-dev-squad"

gh issue create --title "🐛 Build Centipede game" --body "## Game: Centipede (Priority #7)

**Squad:** Game Development  
**Complexity:** ⭐⭐ Medium

### Description
Segmented enemy descends through mushroom field. Player shoots from bottom.

### Requirements
- Single index.html file, Canvas 2D, zero dependencies
- Neo-brutalist styling
- Centipede segments that split on hit
- Mushroom grid obstacles
- Player movement in bottom area + shooting
- localStorage high scores

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Centipede moves and descends through mushrooms
- [ ] Segments split independently when hit
- [ ] Mushrooms block movement, take multiple hits
- [ ] Player can move and shoot
- [ ] Score and high score tracking
- [ ] Neo-brutalist visual style" --label "game,priority-3,game-dev-squad"

gh issue create --title "☄️ Build Asteroids game" --body "## Game: Asteroids (Priority #8)

**Squad:** Game Development  
**Complexity:** ⭐⭐⭐ Medium-Hard

### Description
Vector-based ship with rotation, thrust, and wrap-around. Raw line art = peak neo-brutalist.

### Requirements
- Single index.html file, Canvas 2D, zero dependencies
- Neo-brutalist styling (vector line art, no fills)
- Ship rotation and thrust physics
- Screen wrap-around
- Asteroids split into smaller pieces
- Projectile shooting
- localStorage high scores

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ship rotates, thrusts, and wraps around screen
- [ ] Asteroids drift and split on hit (large → medium → small)
- [ ] Projectiles destroy asteroids
- [ ] Collision detection ship vs asteroids
- [ ] Score and high score tracking
- [ ] Neo-brutalist vector line style" --label "game,priority-3,game-dev-squad"

gh issue create --title "🎯 Build Missile Command game" --body "## Game: Missile Command (Priority #9)

**Squad:** Game Development  
**Complexity:** ⭐⭐⭐ Medium

### Description
Click-to-target with explosion radius. Dramatic negative space, geometric explosions.

### Requirements
- Single index.html file, Canvas 2D, zero dependencies
- Neo-brutalist styling (bold geometric explosions)
- Mouse click targeting
- Missiles travel from bases to click point
- Expanding explosion radius destroys incoming
- Limited ammo per wave
- Cities to defend
- localStorage high scores

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Click launches missile to target point
- [ ] Explosions expand and destroy incoming missiles
- [ ] Cities can be destroyed, game over when all lost
- [ ] Wave progression with increasing difficulty
- [ ] Score and high score tracking
- [ ] Neo-brutalist visual style" --label "game,priority-3,game-dev-squad"

gh issue create --title "👻 Build Pac-Man game" --body "## Game: Pac-Man (Priority #10)

**Squad:** Game Development  
**Complexity:** ⭐⭐⭐ Hard

### Description
Maze navigation with ghost AI. Most complex due to ghost behavior patterns. Saved for last.

### Requirements
- Single index.html file, Canvas 2D, zero dependencies
- Neo-brutalist styling
- Maze generation/rendering
- Pac-Man movement with wall collision
- 4 ghosts with distinct AI behaviors
- Power pellets that reverse chase
- Dot collection and level clear
- localStorage high scores

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pac-Man navigates maze with wall collision
- [ ] 4 ghosts with chase/scatter/frightened modes
- [ ] Dots and power pellets work correctly
- [ ] Level clears when all dots eaten
- [ ] Score and high score tracking
- [ ] Neo-brutalist visual style" --label "game,priority-3,game-dev-squad"

All issues filed. Here's the summary:

## 🎮 Game Development Squad — Roger that! Issues filed! ✅

Loud and clear, boss. **Issues-first workflow adopted.** I just filed issues for all 10 games:

| # | Issue | Game | Priority |
|---|-------|------|----------|
| #18 | 🐍 Snake | ⭐ | P1 |
| #20 | 🏓 Pong | ⭐ | P1 |
| #22 | 🧱 Breakout | ⭐⭐ | P2 |
| #24 | 👾 Space Invaders | ⭐⭐ | P2 |
| #33 | 🐸 Frogger | ⭐⭐ | P2 |
| #34 | 🟦 Tetris | ⭐⭐ | P2 |
| #35 | 🐛 Centipede | ⭐⭐ | P3 |
| #36 | ☄️ Asteroids | ⭐⭐⭐ | P3 |
| #37 | 🎯 Missile Command | ⭐⭐⭐ | P3 |
| #38 | 👻 Pac-Man | ⭐⭐⭐ | P3 |

**Our workflow going forward:**
1. 📋 File issue before starting work
2. 🌿 Branch per game (`feature/snake`, etc.)
3. 🔨 Code → PR referencing the issue
4. 👀 Cross-squad PR reviews welcome
5. ✅ Merge when QA approves

Also created labels (`game`, `priority-1/2/3`, `game-dev-squad`) for easy filtering.

**@site-design-squad, @qa-squad, @research-and-ideation-squad** — We'll review and merge your PRs too. Let's keep the issue board clean and current. 🚀

Ready to start on Snake (#18) whenever the team gives the green light!
